question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a /api/v1/transactions request where the normalized URL produced by normalizeRequestQueryParams() differs from the original URL in parameter order, causing a cache miss for a previously cached response and forcing a fresh database query that returns a different (potentially more recent or stale) transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?block.number=gt:16&block.number=lt:19 vs ?block.number=lt:19&block.number=gt:16 to /api/v1/blocks, exploiting the order-sensitivity of NON_SORTED_PARAMS to receive different block/transaction sets, reorganizing which blocks and their associated transactions are visible?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default value injection in normalizeRequestQueryParams() for the 'internal' parameter (default=false) on /api/v1/contracts/:id/results, causing internal contract transactions to be hidden from the response, reorganizing the visible contract execution history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single-element array for a COLLAPSABLE_PARAM (e.g., ?nonce=1) to /api/v1/transactions/:id, where getNormalizedArrayValue() processes it as an array via slice(valueArray.length - 1), and verify whether the single-element collapse produces the same result as a scalar value, to identify any discrepancy that could reorganize transaction lookup?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send an empty array for a timestamp parameter (e.g., by crafting a request where timestamp resolves to []) to /api/v1/transactions, causing getNormalizedArrayValue() to return undefined (isEmpty check), which drops the timestamp filter entirely and reorganizes the transaction history to return all transactions instead of a filtered set?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /api/v1/tokens/:id/nfts/:serialNumber/transactions with ?timestamp=gte:T1&timestamp=lte:T2 in one order, and ?timestamp=lte:T2&timestamp=gte:T1 in another, exploiting NON_SORTED_PARAMS order preservation to receive different NFT transaction history sets, reorganizing the visible NFT transfer history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?nonce=0&nonce=1 to /api/v1/transactions/:id, exploiting COLLAPSABLE_PARAMS collapse (last value wins: nonce=1), causing the child transaction with nonce=1 to be returned instead of the parent transaction (nonce=0), reorganizing which transaction record is visible for that transaction ID?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that normalizeRequestQueryParams() only processes parameters defined in the OpenAPI spec (openApiMap), causing any extra/unknown parameters to be silently dropped from the normalized URL, which changes the effective query and reorganizes the transaction history returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?timestamp=1639010141.000000001&timestamp=1639010141.000000000 to /api/v1/transactions (as shown in the test: 'Timestamp values are not sorted'), exploiting NON_SORTED_PARAMS order preservation, to receive a different transaction set than ?timestamp=1639010141.000000000&timestamp=1639010141.000000001, reorganizing the visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where the 'path' parameter passed to normalizeRequestQueryParams() contains a trailing slash, causing the returned normalized URL to differ from the canonical form, leading to cache key mismatches that serve stale or incorrect transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?scheduled=true&scheduled=false&scheduled=true to /api/v1/transactions/:id, exploiting COLLAPSABLE_PARAMS collapse (last value wins: scheduled=true), causing the scheduled version of the transaction to be returned, reorganizing which transaction variant appears in the history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?block.hash=0xAAA&block.hash=0xBBB&block.hash=0xCCC to a contracts/results endpoint, exploiting COLLAPSABLE_PARAMS collapse (last value wins: block.hash=0xCCC), causing the query to return contract results from block 0xCCC instead of 0xAAA, reorganizing which block's transaction history is visible?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between normalizeRequestQueryParams() default value injection and the 'order' parameter, where omitting 'order' causes the default (e.g., 'desc') to be injected, changing the sort direction of the transaction history from ascending to descending and reorganizing the visible transaction sequence?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?account.id=gt:0.0.100&account.id=lt:0.0.20 to /api/v1/accounts, exploiting the lexicographic sort of non-NON_SORTED_PARAMS (gt:0.0.100 < lt:0.0.20 lexicographically), causing the SQL query to apply filters in an order that returns a different set of accounts/transactions than the user intended?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /api/v1/contracts/results/logs with ?timestamp=T1&index=lt:1&topic0=A, exploiting the fact that normalizeRequestQueryParams() reorders parameters to match OpenAPI spec order (index, limit, order, timestamp, topic0), causing the normalized URL to differ from the original and potentially hit a different cache entry with different transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that normalizeRequestQueryParams() is called after error handling (as documented in the JSDoc comment), meaning a request that passes validation but has parameters reordered by normalization could produce a different SQL query result, reorganizing the transaction history returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?nonce=2&nonce=1 to /api/v1/transactions/0x{hash}?nonce=2&nonce=1, exploiting COLLAPSABLE_PARAMS collapse (last value wins: nonce=1) on a hash-based transaction lookup, causing the wrong nonce-indexed transaction to be returned and reorganizing the visible transaction history for that hash?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where the query object contains a parameter with value '0' or 'false', and verify whether isEmpty('0') or isEmpty('false') in lodash returns true (it does not for strings), to confirm that these values are correctly preserved in the normalized URL and not silently dropped, which would reorganize the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?timestamp=lt:T&timestamp=gt:T2 to /api/v1/transactions where T < T2 (logically empty range), exploiting NON_SORTED_PARAMS order preservation to produce a normalized URL that the SQL backend interprets as a valid range returning no transactions, effectively hiding all transaction history for that time window?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between normalizeRequestQueryParams() and the pagination link generation in getPaginationLink(), where the normalized URL is used as the base for the 'next' link, causing the next page of transaction history to point to a different query than intended and reorganizing the pagination sequence?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /api/v1/accounts with ?balance=true (explicit) vs omitting balance entirely (causing default injection of balance=true), and verify whether the two normalized URLs are identical, to identify any discrepancy that could cause cache key collisions and serve wrong transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?nonce=1&scheduled=false vs ?scheduled=false&nonce=1 to /api/v1/transactions/:id, exploiting the fact that COLLAPSABLE_PARAMS are processed in OpenAPI spec order (not user order), to verify whether the normalized output differs and causes different transaction lookup behavior, reorganizing the visible transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where the openApiRoute passed to normalizeRequestQueryParams() is undefined (e.g., for a route not matched by the OpenAPI validator), causing isEmpty(openApiParameters) to be true and falling back to querystring.stringify(query), which may produce a different parameter ordering than the original request and reorganize the transaction history query?
